data.content:
Verification steps (beyond AC8 quality gates):

V1. In ~/knots: create a test knot, create a lease with --agent-name claude --model opus --model-version 4.7 --provider anthropic --agent-type code, claim the knot with --lease <id>. Run kno next to transition through planning -> plan_review -> implementation. Dump `kno show <id> --json` and confirm every step_history entry carries agent_name/agent_model/agent_version derived from the lease.
V2. In ~/knots: repeat V1 but call `kno next <id> --agent-name bogus --agent-model bogus --agent-version bogus`. Confirm stderr shows the three-line deprecation warning and that the recorded step_history entry still carries the lease's agent identity, not "bogus".
V3. In ~/knots: call `kno next <id>` on a knot with no bound lease. Confirm stderr shows the warning with the "no lease bound; create one" branch, and the step_history entry is written with unset agent fields.
V4. In ~/knots: repeat V2 for kno update --add-note --note-agentname bogus, kno update --add-handoff-capsule --handoff-agentname bogus, kno gate evaluate --agent-name bogus, kno rollback --agent-name bogus, kno step annotate --agent-name bogus. Same assertion each time.
V5. In ~/foolery: start the dev server, drive a knot through the Foolery UI via the full workflow (poll -> take -> next x N). Open the Retakes screen, expand a beat row, confirm each step shows real "agentname | model | version | username | time" instead of empty fields.
V6. In ~/foolery: grep server logs produced during V5 for "deprecated" -- after the client cleanup, there should be zero deprecation warnings surfacing from kno invocations.
V7. In ~/foolery: run `rg "'--agent-name'|\\\"--agent-name\\\"|--note-agentname|--handoff-agentname" src --type=ts` and confirm the only hit is inside createLease.
